description: SLS(26) SUPERSEDES SLS(25). Two evidence-driven changes, both derived from independent quant analysis of 65k real EURUSD M5 bars. (1) SLS25: Hurst-first veto replaced by MA-fan consensus -- macro EMA200 displacement>=0.8ATR AND 20-bar slope>=0.3ATR NECESSARY, faster voices confirm. Verified: trend recognition 29%->89%, false-trend-in-chop 11%->0%. New globals g_Ema200DistRing[20], g_Ema200DistHist20. (2) SLS26: IC SIGNIFICANCE SHRINKAGE in MeasureRegimeIC. A forward-edge audit (TP2.0/SL1.2 ATR, E[R]=0 under random walk, Bonferroni-corrected) found NO signal in this system has statistically real forward edge -- macro displacement identifies trend perfectly yet earns -0.003R; EMA21-angle and DI/ADX identify trend and LOSE money. Raw Spearman IC on finite samples is never zero, so noise was becoming weight. Now every IC (and the ccy IC) is shrunk by min(1, |ic|/(2*SE)) with SE=1/sqrt(n-3); n<=6 gives IC=0. Cannot manufacture edge, only refuse to. Weights relax to neutral when evidence is thin. Structural parity verified (brace 0, paren identical to base). COMPILE-VERIFY ONLY; report errors, fix minimally, report exact changes.
